Udemy Tip: Translate Subtitles in Real Time With a Translation Extension + JS Snippet

Udemy has tons of courses people want to take, but a lot of them are in English. If you'd rather watch in another language, you can use a browser translation extension to translate the transcript — but the video's subtitle overlay keeps reverting to English on play. This post records a workaround that mirrors the translated transcript to the overlay in real time.
Pick whatever translation extension you like. I use
Open Udemy and pick a course — important: pick one
The transcript panel shows up on the side:
Now use your translation extension to translate the transcript:
The transcript is translated, and even the video's overlay subtitle becomes translated:
But the moment you hit Play — the overlay flips back to the original language:
You can read the translated transcript on the side, but that's annoying — most people watch the overlay at the bottom. I found
that solves this (I rewrote it a bit to avoid noisy errors).
First, click into the subtitle settings and pin the subtitles to the bottom (more reliable):
Then open DevTools (F12 or right-click → Inspect) → Console, and paste this in:

Now the video's overlay subtitle stays in your target language during playback. Also enable auto-scroll on the transcript so it keeps updating as the video plays — that way the substitution loop stays in sync:
In Edge, you can right-click → Translate Page and both the video and page text translate together with sync. Thanks to bucky0112 for sharing.

Q: Why does the substitution sometimes fail?
A: Usually because the transcript panel hasn't been translated yet for that segment. Scroll the transcript panel down — once the panel is fully translated, the substitution starts working properly.

Q: How do I stop the auto-translation?
A: Run
How translated subtitles on Udemy in real time with JS and Google Translate

html pre.shiki code .sWyjQ, html code.shiki .sWyjQ{--shiki-default:#FF9492}html pre.shiki code .sj_SG, html code.shiki .sj_SG{--shiki-default:#DBB7FF}html pre.shiki code .sMAXC, html code.shiki .sMAXC{--shiki-default:#F0F3F6}html pre.shiki code .sCcAr, html code.shiki .sCcAr{--shiki-default:#91CBFF}html pre.shiki code .sTRMh, html code.shiki .sTRMh{--shiki-default:#ADDCFF}html .default .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-default);background: var(--shiki-default-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-default-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-default-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-default-text-decoration);}html .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-default);background: var(--shiki-default-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-default-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-default-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-default-text-decoration);}

Udemy has great courses, but lots of them are in English. If you'd rather watch with subtitles in another language, you can use a browser translation extension to translate the transcript panel — but the video's own subtitle overlay stays in English. This post shares a small JS snippet you can paste into the browser console to mirror the translated transcript onto the video's subtitle overlay in real time.
